item.title: Polymarket’s Latest Pop-Up Is a Sports Bar for Watching the News
item.description: Polymarket took over a bar near the White House, for a promotional event showcasing the prediction market’s vision of betting on current events as a new form of entertainment.

item.title: US Allows Sale of Stranded Iranian Oil to Cap Energy Price Hikes
item.description: The US Department of Treasury issued a general license allowing the sale of Iranian oil and petrochemical products loaded onto vessels on or before 12:01 am New York time Friday, marking the Trump administration’s latest effort to counter rising oil prices due to the Iran war.

item.title: Blackstone Private Credit Fund Has First Monthly Loss Since 2022
item.description: Blackstone Inc.’s flagship private credit fund posted its first monthly loss in more than three years, one of the clearest signs yet of weakening performance in the $1.8 trillion market.

item.title: Netflix Grows Live Event Strategy With BTS Comeback Spectacular
item.description: Netflix Inc. is turning the heart of Seoul into a global stage as K-pop megastars BTS return to performance with a livestream comeback show that is expected to draw millions of viewers worldwide as part of a new drive by the US entertainment powerhouse to show live events that can “pull people together”.

item.title: First US Oil Barrels From Emergency Release to Hit Market
item.description: The first oil from US President Donald Trump’s planned 172 million-barrel emergency release is set to start flowing soon, the latest move in the administration’s battle to keep fuel prices in check.

item.title: Nuclear Energy Firm X-Energy Files for US IPO Despite War
item.description: X-Energy Inc. filed for a US initial public offering, becoming the latest firm to advance public listing plans despite turmoil in the Middle East souring risk sentiment.

item.title: JPMorgan Strategists Cut S&P 500 Target on Iran War Uncertainty
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. strategists cut their price target on the S&P 500 Index, saying the upside potential for risk assets is “more constrained” by a war in the Middle East.

item.title: Activist Litt Withdraws Bid for First Industrial REIT’s Board
item.description: Land & Buildings Investment Management founder Jonathan Litt has withdrawn his nomination to join the board of First Industrial Realty Trust Inc., saying the activist investor can press for change more effectively from outside the boardroom.

item.title: Salary, Pension Reforms to Drain $3.4 Billion From Peru’s Coffers
item.description: Peruvian lawmakers are pushing public salary and pension reforms that may add an extra 12 billions soles ($3.45 billion) in spending over the next year, according to a financial watchdog.

item.title: Ireland Credit Rating Raised by S&P on Strong Fiscal Buffer
item.description: S&P Global Ratings upgraded Ireland, underscoring how the economy has held up despite exposure to global trade upheaval.

item.title: Musk Misled Twitter Investors Before 2022 Buyout, Jury Says
item.description: Elon Musk defrauded Twitter Inc. investors when he disparaged the company in 2022 in an effort to buy the social media platform for a lower price than his original $44 billion bid, a jury concluded.

item.title: Electronic Arts $15 Billion Debt Sale Draws $25 Billion Demand
item.description: Electronic Arts Inc. has attracted about $25 billion of demand from investors for a nearly $15 billion debt offering to fund a buyout of the company, a sign of increased interest from buyers as banks navigate volatile markets to offload debt tied to acquisitions.

item.title: Canada’s TSX Erases 2026 Gains as Gold Price Plunges Amid War
item.description: Canadian stocks erased all of their gains for the year as shares of gold producers tumbled and traders bet that central banks may not rush to cut interest rates due to the inflationary effects of the Iran war.

item.title: Chicago Transit Sues Trump Administration Over Frozen Funds
item.description: The Chicago Transit Authority sued the Trump administration over its freezing of billions of dollars in federal funds intended to modernize and expand the city’s elevated trains and subways.

item.title: Wall Street Angst Mounts as Oil Shock, Fed Freeze Roil Markets
item.description: Since Operation Epic Fury began nearly three weeks ago, markets have broadly clung to a comforting bet: that the disruption to oil supply would be brief, the Strait of Hormuz would reopen, and the Federal Reserve’s monetary-easing cycle would resume. On Friday, that bet broke.

item.title: Does Private Credit Work in ETFs? `Absolutely Not,′ Says DoubleLine
item.description: DoubleLine’s Sherman says private assets don’t belong in open-ended ETFs

item.title: Thiel’s Founders Fund Backs AI Cow Collar Startup at $2 Billion Valuation
item.description: Halter, a startup making artificial intelligence-powered collars for cows, is in talks to raise a new funding round that would double its valuation to more than $2 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Paraguay Holds Key Rate at 5.5%, Signals Neutral Stance
item.description: Paraguay’s central bank kept its benchmark interest rate steady at 5.5%, citing below-target inflation and an economy growing at a slower rate than last year.

item.title: Traders Turn Positive on US Dollar for First Time This Year
item.description: Traders turned positive on the US dollar for the first time this year as war in the Middle East jolts energy costs higher and supports the currency’s status as a haven.

item.title: Argentina Economy Grew Less Than Expected in Fourth Quarter
item.description: Argentina’s economy expanded less than analysts anticipated at the end of 2025, marking a slowdown even as President Javier Milei tries to stimulate growth.

item.title: Lula Says Petrobras, Pemex Could Partner on Oil Exploration
item.description: Brazil President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva said he spoke with Mexican leader Claudia Sheinbaum about a possible exploration partnership between their nations’ state-run oil companies in the Gulf of Mexico.

item.title: Markets Raise Bets on Bank of Canada Hikes as Oil Fears Mount
item.description: Markets see the Bank of Canada hiking interest rates more aggressively this year amid surging oil prices and hawkish messaging from peer central banks.

item.title: Central Banker Sees ‘Major Threat’ to Colombia From Iran War
item.description: The war in the Middle East is a “major threat” to a Colombian economy, which already faced serious price pressures, according to a senior policymaker.

item.title: Kalshi Temporarily Banned From Operating in Nevada by State Judge
item.description: Kalshi Inc. was temporarily barred by a judge from offering its prediction market contracts in Nevada, after state regulators said the company didn’t have a gaming license.

item.title: Anduril Bets on Simple Factory to Mass-Produce Uncrewed Fighters
item.description: Anduril Industries is ready to roll its first uncrewed Fury fighter off a production line in Ohio this summer, producing the computer-brained plane with a novel techniques: made-by-hand assembly without complicated machinery or robotics.

item.title: The Oil Prices You See Don’t Tell the Market’s Real Story
item.description: Three weeks into the Iran war, there’s an ever-growing gap between the price of oil futures and supplies that determine costs for consumers in the real world.

item.title: Tanker of Russian Fuel Veers Away From Cuba as US Tweaks Rules
item.description: A tanker carrying a cargo of diesel believed to be bound for Cuba updated its destination to Trinidad and Tobago shortly after the US clarified that the communist-run island remains ineligible to receive Russian fuel.

item.title: Muni Market Rout Deepens as Iran War Stokes Inflation Concerns
item.description: Pressure is building in the US municipal bond market.

item.title: Italian Bonds Emerge as Europe’s Weak Spot on Carry Trade Unwind
item.description: Italy’s bonds have been hammered since the start of the Iran war, emerging as the weakest link in the euro area as investors unwind one of the market’s favorite trades.

item.title: Janus Living Shares Jump About 18% After $840 Million IPO
item.description: Janus Living Inc., a seniors-focused real estate investment trust, jumped nearly 18% in its trading debut Friday after the company raised $840 million in an initial public offering.
